label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so Alice and Bob are playing this game with tokens. The rules are: they take turns removing either 1 or 4 tokens from a stack. Alice goes first, and whoever takes the last token wins. We need to find how many positive integers n (up to 2024) are such that Bob can guarantee a win no matter what Alice does. First, I need to figure out for each n, whether it's a winning position or a losing position. A losing position means that no matter what move the current player makes, the opponent can force a win. Conversely, a winning position means the current player can make a move that forces the opponent into a losing position. Since Alice goes first, if n is a losing position,
